segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a dynamic, fast-paced transition, revealing a vibrant, futuristic esports arena bathed in dramatic red and blue lighting, setting the stage for the high-stakes finals between WBG and T1. Commentators immediately build excitement, noting the 'incredible, incredible stuff' and highlighting that 'game one' is about to begin, with WBG (referred to as Weibo) having 'selected blue side.' The camera pans across the WBG team, seated in black jerseys adorned with 'ifland' and 'R.GG' logos, intensely focused on their screens, wearing headsets. The scene shifts to the T1 team, in white jerseys with red accents, equally concentrated, as commentators describe them as 'absolutely a terror on red side.' Close-ups of individual players, including TheShy, Weiwei, and Faker, emphasize their intense expressions and strategic focus. A large, glowing blue portal-like structure with the championship trophy at its center dominates the background, underscoring the grandeur. As the draft phase progresses, character selections are revealed on-screen. Commentators discuss 'what is Weibo's plan going to be in game number one,' especially as they are 'the underdogs.' A key revelation is made: '100% Faker's playing a different champion this time around,' a significant 'discussion point' regarding mid-lane focus. WBG picks Maokai and Kolista, while T1 selects Renata Glasc and other champions. The commentators note 'Maokai left available' and that 'Rell is available for Oner and he has been stellar on it.' The 'Kalista has been such incredible priority,' and 'Renata Glasc is the most important champion for this series as a whole.' The atmosphere remains electric with dramatic lighting and visual effects. Wide shots capture both teams' branded desks with 'WBG' and 'T1' logos. Commentators further discuss 'Crisp, incredible on the champion' and 'Keria of course debuting it in their very first game in the Swiss stage at Worlds.' The segment concludes with a focus on the players' reactions and the evolving draft, underscoring the competitive intensity, as commentators emphasize the importance of understanding 'what the rest of Weibo's plan is.'

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of WBG top laner TheShy, intensely focused with his hand near his mouth, wearing a white jersey with red accents and glasses, as commentators discuss denying Crisp a pick and Keria's extremely successful play on Renata and Kalista. The camera then pans across the WBG team – Weiwei, Xiaohu, Light, and Crisp – all in black jerseys with 'ifland' and 'R.GG' logos, seated at their red-lit desk, deeply concentrated. The Draft Interface updates in real-time, displaying champion picks and bans, as a commentator announces TheShy will go back to Aatrox but immediately pulls out his Yone, and Xiaohu will be playing the Jayce, forming the "Malphite-Jayce combo that has terrorized the rift all year." The scene shifts to a wide shot of the grand stage, featuring the Championship Trophy at the center, illuminated by a glowing blue portal-like structure between the WBG and T1 team desks, while a commentator expresses excitement for "game one in the finals" with T1 "getting spicy already." The camera then focuses on the T1 team – Zeus, Oner, Faker, Gumayusi, and Keria – all in white jerseys with red accents, seated at their blue-lit desk, with a close-up of Faker's serious expression. Commentators briefly question if Yone is for Zeus, then extensively praise Faker, highlighting his "70th unique champion," his status as a "consummate professional," "such a grinder," and his ability to "play everything." The Draft Interface continues to update with evolving champion selections. The video concludes with a wide-angle view of the entire stage, capturing the grand scale of the Finals with the audience visible in the background, the large screens displaying the players, and the vibrant blue and red lighting, as commentators shift focus to the bot lane.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with a wide shot of the grand esports arena, showcasing the dramatic stage design centered around a glowing blue portal and the Championship Trophy. The WBG and T1 teams are seated at their respective red and blue-lit desks, with the Draft Interface prominently displayed at the bottom, showing the ongoing champion selection for the Finals match. Commentators discuss T1's strategic bans, specifically targeting bot lane champions like Varus and Ashe, and analyze Crisp's expanded champion pool, noting his recent shift towards enchanters like Lux and Heimerdinger. The camera then cuts to close-ups of the T1 players, focusing on Faker, who is intensely focused with his glasses reflecting the stage lights, and Zeus, who appears concentrated. The commentators then shift their focus to WBG's draft strategy, highlighting how their bans have targeted Oner's best champions, including Rell, J4, and Sejuani, potentially putting him in a difficult position. The scene shifts to a wider view of the T1 team in their white jerseys, then pans to the WBG team – TheShy, Weiwei, Xiaohu, Light, and Crisp – in their black jerseys, all deeply engaged in the draft. The Draft Interface updates in real-time, displaying pick/ban statistics and win rates. The atmosphere is tense and electric, with the audience in darkness and the stage illuminated by dynamic blue lighting, emphasizing the high stakes. The commentators grow increasingly excited as Faker's Ahri pick is confirmed, discussing its implications and potential pairings like Vi, or even Poppy, while also mentioning a potential Senna TK pick to neutralize the opponent. The camera alternates between close-ups of individual players and wide shots of the stage, capturing the intensity and focus of both teams as they finalize their champion selections.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on the intense draft phase of the League of Legends Finals, with commentators immediately discussing bottom lane strategy. WBG players—TheShy, Weiwei, Xiaohu, Light, and Crisp—are seen intently focused at their red-lit desk, wearing black jerseys, as commentators highlight the potential for Light and Crisp to revert to a familiar playstyle. TheShy, in a white jersey and glasses, is captured in a close-up, hand near his mouth, displaying deep concentration. The camera then shifts to T1 at their blue-lit desk, showing Zeus, Oner, Faker, Gumayusi, and Keria in white jerseys, capturing Faker's serious expression through his glasses. The Draft Interface updates in real-time, displaying champion bans and picks, as commentators ponder options like Tahm Kench, noting its difficulty as a single-target engage, and suggest Poppy as a potential pick for Oner. The tension escalates dramatically as Lee Sin is picked, eliciting an excited "Oh my god! Lee Sin!" from the commentators, who then confirm it as Oner's "most famous champion by a country mile." The stage backdrop, featuring a massive, glowing blue portal-like structure with the Championship Trophy at its center, is illuminated by dynamic lighting shifts between blue and red, mirroring the competitive atmosphere. Commentators further analyze T1's strategy, noting their previous team-fighting approach against JDG and emphasizing the current composition's need to secure an early lead, with Faker, Oner, Gumayusi, and Keria needing to stack dragons and get ahead before the mid-to-late game. The segment concludes with a wide shot of the grand stage, the trophy gleaming, as the teams are poised for the match to begin.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a cinematic flourish, showcasing the grand Championship Trophy—a large, silver, ornate cup with a glowing blue gem—set against a dark, mystical stage with floating debris and a blue portal, as the camera pans to emphasize its grandeur. This transitions to a wide shot of the vibrant, fantasy-themed game map, featuring rocky terrain, glowing 'THE GRIND' banners, and a central green emblem. The scene then shifts to the live in-game match between WBG and T1, with the scoreboard showing 0-0 and 2.5K gold for each team. Two commentators immediately dive into the strategy, with one exclaiming, "game number one! Let's do it!" amidst crowd cheers. The first commentator points out, "already, I've got to point out it's triple TP for Weibo," specifically noting that "Crisp is going to be taking the TP on that Tom Kench," discussing the advantage of this extra teleport for farming and mid-to-late game. The camera follows WBG's Crisp and Light as their champions cautiously advance through the river and jungle, while T1's Keria and Gumayusi position defensively. The action quickly moves to the river where Weiwei, at level 4, secures First Blood on T1's Oner, also level 4, near the blue side river entrance, triggering a level-up for TheShy to level 6. An XP+LVL overlay briefly shows T1's Faker and WBG's TheShy both at level 7. As T1's Keria and Gumayusi move into the river to assist, WBG's Crisp and Light maintain pressure. Simultaneously, the commentators detail Oner clearing vision with a control ward in the Drake pit, anticipating the first dragon, a 'Kentech Drake,' at around the six-minute mark. Faker is seen coming over, gathering information but unable to steal the objective. T1 successfully secures the first dragon at 6:04, marked by the on-screen notification 'Red team has slain the Dragon!' and the distinct sound of the dragon's defeat. The commentators emphasize this as a crucial 'step' for T1 to gain 'big advantages,' noting Gumayusi's 'huge advantage' over the enemy Tam Kench. Following the dragon take, teams disengage, with WBG's Light and Crisp pushing the mid-lane minion wave while T1's Gumayusi and Keria retreat to their base. Throughout, the bottom-left player feeds show WBG players in black jerseys with 'ifland' and 'R.GG' logos, while T1 players wear white jerseys with red accents in the bottom-right feeds. The minimap tracks T1's convergence on the dragon pit, all set against the game's stone pathways, glowing 'THE GRIND' banners, and a central green emblem, with the aws logo and Prime Gaming advertisement visible. The score remains 1-0 for WBG, with both teams at 0 kills/deaths and around 9K gold, accompanied by consistent esports commentary and in-game sounds of abilities and pings.

Commentators continue to analyze strategies, emphasizing the importance of early game advantages like stacking dragons and using dragon soul pressure to force opponents into disadvantageous fights.

segments.integrated_caption: The League of Legends match between WBG and T1 unfolds from 7:22 to 8:12, with two commentators providing dynamic play-by-play and strategic analysis throughout the segment. In the top lane, WBG's TheShy, whose champion Aatrox is noted by commentators as being "perm-pushed in," engages T1's Zeus, who commentators observe "keeps shoving these waves relentlessly into TheShy's turret." Despite this pressure, TheShy, at level 7, secures a kill on Zeus, highlighted by an 'UNSTOPPABLE' kill feed as Zeus retreats. Simultaneously in mid lane, Xiaohu and Faker trade blows, both leveling up to 7, with commentators noting Faker "almost" has his flash available while Xiaohu "already has his," contributing to Xiaohu gaining an advantage. The river becomes a critical battleground as WBG's Weiwei and Crisp push toward T1's Oner and Keria. Commentators discuss T1's strategy to use "top lane prio to secure Herald" and that Gumayusi and Keria have "already rotated over." This leads to a skirmish where Weiwei secures first blood on Oner. T1's Gumayusi and Keria retreat cautiously, while WBG's Light and Crisp maintain pressure near the river. The minimap shows T1's jungle presence moving toward the river, while WBG coordinates a push. The score stands at 1-0 for WBG, with gold nearly even at 12.6K for WBG and 12.9K for T1, though commentators perceive T1 as having a slight gold advantage after a "first plate has gone down already here for T1." The bottom-left player feeds show WBG players in black jerseys with 'ifland' and 'R.GG' logos, intensely focused, while T1 players in white jerseys with red accents are seen in the bottom-right feeds, with Faker and Keria showing focused expressions. The environment features stone pathways, glowing 'THE GRIND' banners, and river terrain, with the OPPO and AWS logos visible, indicating sponsorships.

As the fight erupts, WBG’s Weiwei engages Oner aggressively, securing first blood after a quick burst of damage. The skirmish intensifies as WBG’s Light and Crisp push forward, while T1’s Keria and Faker attempt to reposition. WBG’s Xiaohu levels up to 6 during the chaos, gaining an advantage in the mid-lane skirmish. T1’s Oner is eliminated, and the fight shifts toward the river’s center, where WBG’s Crisp is taken down by T1’s Oner and Keria. The battle continues with WBG’s TheShy and Weiwei engaging T1’s Zeus and Oner in the top river, leading to TheShy securing a kill on Zeus. TheShy then levels up to 6, and the teams disengage, with WBG retreating to regroup while T1 maintains control of the river. The minimap shows both teams’ movements, with T1’s Faker and Gumayusi retreating to the mid-lane, while WBG’s Light and Crisp reposition near the bottom lane. The score remains 1-1, with T1 holding a slight gold advantage. The bottom-left player feeds show WBG players in black jerseys with 'ifland' and 'R.GG' logos, intensely focused, while T1 players in white jerseys with red accents are visible in the bottom-right feeds, displaying focused expressions. The Prime Gaming advertisement remains at the bottom, and the game map environment features glowing 'THE GRIND' banners and stone pathways. The atmosphere is tense, with both teams vying for control of the river and map objectives.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens in a dynamic early-game phase of a League of Legends match between WBG and T1, with the score tied at 1-1 and gold nearly even, as commentators discuss farm on the bot side and Crisp's lack of TP. The action quickly escalates in the top lane where WBG's TheShy, playing a melee champion, engages T1's Zeus. Commentators describe Zeus's "Nature's Grasp" coming in but being "pulled back" and "interrupted from the ultimate," noting he "does get a knock up there and a shield but I think he's still dead." Indeed, TheShy successfully secures a kill after landing a stun and follow-up damage, with commentators confirming "the answering kill from TheShy," increasing WBG's kill count to 2-1 and allowing "Weibo [WBG] to punch back on the top side." Simultaneously, in the river area, WBG's Weiwei and Crisp coordinate with Light to pressure T1's bottom lane, while T1's Gumayusi and Keria retreat cautiously, having "taken a plate themselves down there." Commentators highlight that TheShy's "great kill back on the top side does help to equalize the gold there," noting "gold now actually completely fine" and that TheShy "might even be able to pick up a plate." Crucially, they point out that "Zeus doesn't have TP," meaning he "is going to lose out on this wave," unlike Faker who "just TP'd back to the lane" earlier. The mid lane sees Xiaohu and Faker trading blows, with Xiaohu leveling up to 6 and gaining an advantage. TheShy continues to push his lane aggressively, while Crisp and Light secure the first tower in the bottom lane. T1's Oner and Keria attempt to contest the river, but WBG maintains control, with Crisp using his abilities to secure vision and engage. Commentators explain that "Zeus just couldn't use that ultimate quite fast enough," and "Weiwei was able to get ahead of him, blocks the ulti," leading to Zeus's demise. They add that Zeus, realizing he was "dead either way," decided to "hold on to the flash," calling it an "unfortunate death for him as he had so much pressure on top side, so much tempo." The map shows WBG's champions pushing lanes and securing objectives, while T1's team repositions defensively. The bottom-left player feeds show WBG players in black jerseys with 'ifland' and 'R.GG' logos, intensely focused, while T1 players in white jerseys with red accents are seen in the bottom-right feeds, reacting to the skirmishes. The Red Bull advertisement 'GIVES YOU WIINGS.' and the Prime Gaming banner remain visible in the bottom-left corner, and the aws logo appears at 4:02. The game environment features stone pathways, glowing 'THE GRIND' banners, and a river with jungle terrain, under consistent in-game lighting.

segments.integrated_caption: In a high-intensity early-game phase of the League of Legends match between WBG and T1, with the game clock around 10:20–11:20, WBG gains significant momentum. The action kicks off in the top lane as WBG's TheShy (level 9) trades blows with T1's Zeus (level 9) near the stone pathways and 'THE GRIND' banners, with TheShy leveling up to 10 and gaining an advantage. Simultaneously, in the mid lane, WBG's Xiaohu (level 9) engages T1's Faker (level 9), who also levels up to 10 during the skirmish, but Xiaohu ultimately gains the upper hand and pushes the lane. Commentators highlight Faker's 'relentless aggression' and his ability to 'bounce back' despite being the 'most ganked man of all time.' The focus then shifts to the river where WBG's Weiwei (level 7) and Crisp (level 7) coordinate with TheShy to pressure T1's Oner (level 7) and Keria (level 6), leading to a skirmish where Weiwei secures first blood on Oner. TheShy, now empowered, turns his attention back to Zeus, who is caught out of position, securing a second kill for WBG. As T1's Gumayusi (level 6) and Keria (level 6) retreat cautiously, commentators note their 'reset' and the importance of their next item. TheShy, now at level 10, continues to dominate the top lane, pushing toward the turret. The minimap shows T1's champions retreating to their base while WBG maintains pressure, with commentators observing 'Weibo is moving up' and discussing the pivotal second dragon, identified as a Cloud Drake. The game score stands at 2-1 in favor of WBG, with gold nearly even at 17.6K–18.5K. WBG players, in black jerseys with 'ifland' and 'R.GG' logos, are intensely focused in the bottom-left feeds, while T1 players, in white jerseys with red accents, react in the bottom-right. The environment features glowing torches, stone pathways, and jungle terrain, with the 'Secret Lab' logo visible in the bottom-left and the 'aws' logo appearing as a sponsor.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with two male commentators discussing team mobility and WBG's potential to contest, as WBG’s jungler Weiwei and mid laner Xiaohu engage T1’s jungler Oner and mid laner Faker near the river, initiating a small fight. Visually, TheShy, WBG’s top laner, pushes his lane before rotating towards the river, while Zeus, T1’s top laner, moves to join the intensifying skirmish. Commentator 1 notes that 'Prior is now being found by Light and Crisp,' as WBG’s ADC Light and support Crisp maintain cautious positioning. WBG secures first blood on Oner after a coordinated engage, accompanied by in-game kill sounds. T1 retreats, but quickly regroups to secure the first dragon, with Oner and Keria leading the fight. The in-game announcer declares, 'T1 has slain the Dragon!' amidst a dragon roar and gold sounds, with the game clock advancing to 12:00. Commentator 1 observes Faker 'sticking around for the cash' and gathering it, but not for another plate, while Weiwei goes for a reset. Commentator 2 adds that 'Oner drops the Herald mid,' and with Keria and Gumayusi being 'such a strong point early on,' T1 re-establishes priority on the bot side and moves up through the river, securing what Commentator 2 identifies as a 'Cloud Soul.' Following the dragon fight, T1’s ADC Gumayusi and support Keria retreat to the bottom lane. Simultaneously, TheShy levels up to 10, marked by a level-up sound, and secures a kill on Zeus in the top lane. The minimap shows both teams repositioning, with WBG focusing on mid-lane pressure and T1 consolidating after the dragon. Throughout, the bottom-left player feeds show WBG players in black jerseys with 'ifland' and 'R.GG' logos, while the bottom-right feeds show T1 players, including Faker with glasses, focused on the game. The in-game environment features the river, jungle, and stone pathways with glowing 'THE GRIND' banners, and the 'verizon' logo is visible in the bottom-left corner. The score remains 2-1 in favor of WBG, with gold nearly even at 19.9K to 20.1K, maintaining a tense and strategic atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: In a tense early-mid game of League of Legends between WBG and T1, with WBG leading 2-1 and gold nearly even, the action explodes across the map, punctuated by excited commentator analysis and in-game ability sounds. In the top lane, TheShy (WBG) aggressively trades with Zeus (T1), leveling up to 10 and gaining an advantage. Simultaneously, in the river, commentators exclaim, "Oner is here though, it's going to be a 2v2!" as Weiwei (WBG) engages Oner (T1), securing first blood with a quick burst. The commentators continue, "Weiwei punches him out of it! The Dawning Shadow Blade through! To help get the kill on to Zayas!" as TheShy follows up, killing Zeus, pushing the score to 3-1. Mid-lane sees Xiaohu (WBG) level to 10 and pressure Faker (T1) into retreat, while in the bottom lane, Crisp (WBG) is noted by commentators as "taking some damage here, towards the bottom side," as he and Light (WBG) cautiously advance near the river. Light later makes a crucial save, prompting the shout, "And Light saves the play!" T1's Gumayusi and Keria maintain vision, with Keria "looking for an angle, won't find it" as they attempt to burst down Faker. WBG's coordinated movement, visible on the minimap with Crisp and Weiwei rotating top, disrupts T1's jungle control. The bottom-left player feeds show focused WBG players in black 'ifland' and 'R.GG' jerseys, contrasting with the reacting T1 players in white jerseys with red accents in the bottom-right. Sponsor logos like 'verizon', 'aws', and 'WORLDS 2023 EVENT PASS' are visible on-screen amidst the glowing 'THE GRIND' banners and consistent in-game lighting.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with commentators discussing Faker's ultimate and Xiaohu's strategic play, as the tense mid-game phase unfolds with WBG leading T1 3-1 and gold nearly even at 22.1K to 22.3K. Despite T1's Zeus, in a white jersey, maintaining a "big farm lead," the top side skirmishes are favoring WBG. Commentators highlight WBG's Weiwei, also in a black jersey, as a "crucial factor" with "100% kill participation," preventing T1 from gaining momentum. As the game clock progresses from 13:28 to 14:15, commentators discuss T1's dragon stacking and WBG's potential to scale into the mid-to-late game. The tension escalates as commentators warn that TheShy "might be getting ganked." T1's Keria moves up, and Zeus attempts to "break those chains" during the skirmish. TheShy takes significant damage, and the commentators exclaim, "Elko Gaming for TheShy and he will go down. Oner grabs that kill!" Simultaneously, the on-screen text announces, "T1 Oner has slain WBG TheShy!" This pivotal moment, where T1's Oner, seen in a white jersey, secures the kill on TheShy, shifts the momentum. The minimap shows T1's Faker and Gumayusi retreating after a mid-lane skirmish, while WBG's Xiaohu and Light maintain cautious positioning. The environment features the river, stone pathways, and glowing 'THE GRIND' banners, with the AWS logo and 'WORLDS 2023 EVENT PASS' graphic visible.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with intense game sounds and excited commentary as WBG and T1 clash in a pivotal mid-game skirmish near the blue side river, the game clock advancing from 14:23 to 15:20. WBG's Xiaohu, in a black jersey, and T1's Faker, wearing glasses in a white jersey, trade blows in the mid-lane, both champions leveling up to 10. Simultaneously, WBG's Weiwei, in a black jersey, and T1's Oner, in a white jersey, engage in the river, with commentators exclaiming "Weiwei gets out the punch!" as Weiwei secures first blood on Oner, who "is going to burn down." The fight escalates with more game sounds and commentary, as WBG's Crisp and Light, both in black jerseys, join the fray. T1's Keria, in a white jersey, is noted by commentators as "on the roam up towards top side" before he and Gumayusi, also in a white jersey, attempt to reposition. WBG's coordinated pressure leads to multiple kills, including a "SHUT DOWN!" on Keria, with commentators highlighting Crisp's "denial with the devour" and Light briefly being "in a bit of trouble." A LANE ECONOMY SNAPSHOT graphic appears, confirming WBG's mid-lane dominance with Xiaohu leading in gold. The commentators declare "T1 have to run away" and are "getting out skirmished," praising "what a play from Weibo" as WBG walks "away victorious again." Following the river fight, WBG's TheShy, in a white jersey with red accents and glasses, is seen killing T1's Zeus, in a white jersey, in the top lane, despite an earlier commentator's remark about TheShy potentially dying with no TP. The bottom-left player feeds show WBG players (Crisp visible) intensely focused in their black jerseys, while T1 players (Keria visible) in white jerseys are seen in the bottom-right feeds, their strategic positioning revealed on the minimap. WBG pushes towards the enemy jungle while T1 retreats to defend their structures, with WBG maintaining a slight gold advantage and a 4-2 kill lead. Commentators continue to laud Weiwei's "incredible journey as a player" and his transformative impact on WBG. Sponsorship logos for 'aws' and 'verizon' are visible, along with a 'WORLDS 2023 EVENT PASS' graphic.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a 'REPLAY' banner, showcasing a pivotal river skirmish near the blue side’s dragon pit during the Worlds 2023 match between WBG and T1. segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with two male casters discussing the tense mid-game skirmish in the river area of the League of Legends map, where the score is tied at 4-2 in favor of WBG, with both teams having nearly identical gold around 29K. WBG, in black jerseys with 'ifland' and 'R.GG' logos, is seen intensely focused in the bottom-left player feeds, while T1, in white jerseys with red accents, appears stressed in the bottom-right. The casters highlight WBG's refusal to give up the river, with jungler Weiwei "standing stalwart" as he and support Crisp push into the river. T1's jungler Oner and ADC Gumayusi are engaged near the blue side river entrance, with casters noting "Q gonna connect from Oner" as T1 maintains pressure. WBG's mid laner Xiaohu and top laner TheShy, whose champion is seen leveling up to 11, position to support, with casters remarking "TheShy's going to turn up" and "forcing the response from TheShy." T1's mid laner Faker, wearing glasses, and support Keria attempt to counter, but Faker is "probably not wanting to go in," while casters exclaim "Keria's going to miss the handshake!" The casters also track T1's top laner Zeus, who was "very close to that bottom wave" and "did the gromp" before moving back to the bottom lane, pushing it out. As the fight escalates, Weiwei lands a critical engage on Oner, securing first blood after a burst of damage from Crisp and Xiaohu, with casters detailing "Oner not going to go in as the Everfrost, it gets the devour!" TheShy follows up by engaging Zeus, who is caught out of position, and secures his kill. T1's remaining members, including Faker and Gumayusi, retreat under heavy pressure, with casters noting "Gumayusi getting himself out of there and utilizing his ultimate to save Keria as well." The skirmish ends with WBG gaining a 2-0 kill advantage and controlling the river, while T1’s champions are scattered and low on health. The casters conclude by stating "Weibo have started up the Drake." The minimap shows WBG clustered in the river, T1 retreating, and advertisements for Prime Gaming (promoting a Braum emote) and OPPO are visible, alongside glowing 'THE GLORY' banners and a central green emblem in the game environment.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with commentators discussing WBG's potential moves, specifically mentioning "TheShy and Weiwei's ultimate" as cooldowns used, while T1 considers an alternative play, possibly pushing mid. The action quickly intensifies near the river as WBG’s jungler Weiwei and support Crisp engage T1’s jungler Oner. A commentator exclaims, "Oh no! There's a kick flash!" as WBG's mid laner Xiaohu is pushed towards T1's Faker, though a crucial charm misses, and Xiaohu "just walks it off," showing "no worries there at all." T1 secures the first dragon, despite a commentator's initial misstatement of "Weibo get the dragon." The fight escalates with T1's coordinated pressure; a commentator notes "Zayus [Zeus] has found two with the ultimate!" and describes Light going "down so low but isn't going to survive!" Oner secures first blood on Weiwei, followed by Gumayusi killing TheShy, and Oner eliminating Weiwei again, with a commentator confirming "Weiwei, he's going home in the body bag!" and "that is the final kill there from Oner." WBG's Crisp is seen under a turret as T1 destroys WBG’s first turret, gaining map control. TheShy, after being killed, repositions to the top lane and levels up to 11, while Zeus pushes aggressively. The bottom lane remains contested, with Light and Crisp retreating. The scoreboard shows T1 gaining a kill advantage (4-5) and gold lead (32.7K to 30.6K), with the minimap revealing T1’s strategic river convergence. Player feeds show WBG in black jerseys and T1 in white, while various advertisements like Prime Gaming, Red Bull, Secret Lab, aws, Verizon, OPPO, and the 'WORLDS 2023 EVENT PASS' graphic are visible amidst the dynamic lighting of the river, jungle, and 'THE GLORY' banners. Commentators conclude that "T1 have won" the fight, but discuss their "dragon stacking" strategy.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with a replay of a pivotal team fight in the river area during a League of Legends match at Worlds 2023, with the 'REPLAY' label prominently displayed. Commentators discuss T1's 3.3K gold lead, questioning if it's enough for their early game composition, and noting that 'Oner's Lee has been feared.' The action unfolds as T1 and WBG clash near the river, with T1 securing the Rift Herald and initiating a push. Commentators describe 'another miraculous survival' as Xiaohu is able to walk off a play, and Faker doesn't get the chase because of Crisp, who was 'saving his midlaner.' However, it's Zeus, referred to as 'Zayus,' who was 'put down in lane' by TheShy, making a 'hero play.' Despite this, Light is left 'vulnerable' and 'punished big time' as T1 Zeus slays WBG Light, followed by T1 Gumayusi eliminating WBG TheShy, leading to the destruction of WBG’s first turret. Commentators attribute this to T1's 'lane dominance,' with Faker taking down a top tier one turret on the live game. The camera then cuts to the players’ reactions: T1’s Gumayusi is seen celebrating with a hand gesture, while WBG’s Light looks downcast. The live game view resumes, showing the scoreboard with T1 leading 5-4 in kills and 35.5K gold to WBG’s 32.2K. Player feeds are visible for both teams, and commentators express hope that T1 can 'get this mid-game sorted out,' mentioning Oner stealing Raptors and 'full vision given over to Weibo.' The environment features the river, jungle, and stone pathways with glowing 'THE GLORY' banners, while sponsor logos including 'Secret Lab', 'aws', 'verizon', and 'OPPO', and the 'WORLDS 2023 EVENT PASS' graphic remain visible.

segments.integrated_caption: The League of Legends match between WBG and T1 unfolds with T1 leading 4-5 in kills and 35.6K gold to WBG's 32.9K. The action kicks off in the top lane as TheShy (WBG), intensely focused in his white jersey and glasses, engages Zeus (T1), trading damage and leveling up to 11 before securing a kill. Simultaneously, in the mid-lane, Xiaohu (WBG), in his black jersey, exchanges blows with Faker (T1), gaining an advantage and leveling to 10, though commentators note Xiaohu is "in still a lot of trouble." The audio describes Crisp (WBG) "coming forward" to save his mid laner, using a "Safeguard" and later a "TP," but it's a significant investment. The focus then shifts to the river where a large team fight erupts. Weiwei (WBG) clashes with Oner (T1), with Weiwei securing first blood on Oner, as commentators mention "Oner is going to come through as well." The fight intensifies, and Crisp is stunned and eliminated, followed by Light (WBG) being taken down by Oner. TheShy and Zeus rejoin the fray, with TheShy securing another kill on Zeus, while commentators discuss how "Zeus isn't able to actually clip Xiaohu." Faker then eliminates Weiwei. T1 gains a 2-0 kill advantage in the skirmish, and the blue turret in the top lane is destroyed, increasing T1’s tower count to 3. Commentators highlight the "whole breaker" being done, making a champion "such a menace in the side lane" and how it makes "cannon minions" incredibly tanky. The minimap shows T1’s champions converging on the river, while WBG’s team is scattered, retreating after the losses. Towards the end of the minute, commentators note "Guma could be in a bit of trouble here as Crisp looks for it," describing a "cleanse" and abilities like "Nature's Wrath" and "Twisted Advance," followed by a "Hostile Takeover across everyone." TheShy is described as "just gigantic" as Gumayusi (T1) is "trying to hop away," with the call "The fate's gone!" The bottom-left player feeds show WBG players in black jerseys with 'ifland' and 'R.GG' logos, intensely focused, while T1 players in white jerseys with red accents are seen in the bottom-right feeds, displaying calm composure. The environment features the river, jungle, and stone pathways with glowing 'THE GLORY' banners. Sponsor logos for Verizon, Secret Lab, and AWS are visible, along with the game’s HUD displaying gold, kills, and item builds. The atmosphere is tense, with the fight’s outcome shifting momentum toward T1.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with intense commentary describing a pivotal team fight in the river during a high-stakes Worlds 2023 match between WBG and T1. Visually, the action begins with T1's Oner engaging WBG's Weiwei, leading to a chaotic exchange. The commentators exclaim, "Weiwei's going down!" as T1 secures first blood on Weiwei. segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a replay of a chaotic river skirmish during the Worlds 2023 match between WBG and T1, where commentators describe the action near the river’s edge. T1’s Oner secures first blood by eliminating WBG’s Light, an event the commentators highlight as Oner "knocking him down" after Light was "forced to even flash out." Faker then takes down Weiwei, and the fight escalates as T1’s Zeus secures a kill on Xiaohu, leading to a double kill for T1. Commentators emphasize Gumayusi's crucial role, noting how "Guma allows them to arrive" and "dances out, using that ultimate with Keria's help to peel them off." The scene transitions to live gameplay, showing the teams repositioning after the skirmish. T1 maintains a slight gold and kill advantage (10 kills to 5) and is pushing toward Baron Nashor, indicated by the 'BARON POWER PLAY' graphic. Commentators delve into strategic analysis, discussing the challenges of playing "front to back" against a Senna and the difficulty of taking down opponents "particularly with K'Sante." They also analyze WBG's strategy, noting that "they weren't playing front to back League of Legends" and praising TheShy's aggressive play, stating he "goes in is in the middle and it felt fantastic." The camera follows the teams as they maneuver through the jungle and river, with WBG attempting to contest while T1 controls vision and positioning. The bottom-left player feeds show WBG’s TheShy, Weiwei, Xiaohu, Light, and Crisp, while T1’s Zeus, Oner, Faker, Gumayusi, and Keria are visible in the bottom-right. The minimap reveals T1’s coordinated movement toward Baron, with WBG’s champions scattered across the map. The environment features glowing 'THE GLORY' banners and stone pathways, with the river’s dark waters reflecting vibrant spell effects. Sponsor logos for aws, Red Bull, and OPPO are visible, along with the 'WORLDS 23' branding, 'WORLDS 2023 EVENT PASS' graphic, and 'Secret Lab' and 'Verizon' logos. The players remain intensely focused, with close-ups of TheShy and Zeus during a top-lane exchange where TheShy levels to 11 and kills Zeus, and Faker and Xiaohu in a mid-lane skirmish where Xiaohu levels to 10 and gains an advantage. The overall atmosphere is tense and strategic as both teams prepare for the upcoming Baron fight, with commentators concluding that while "so many things did go well for them in the early stages," "when T1 hit, T1 hit way harder."

Casters remark on "Zayus" (Zeus) being "completely back in the game and sitting on a pretty ludicrous gold advantage" despite his demise, while also detailing TheShy's significant power spike, having just finished an item (likely Sterak's Gage) and possessing an Infinity Edge and Kraken Slayer. The camera follows the teams as they reposition, with WBG’s Light and Crisp advancing cautiously near the river. The minimap shows T1’s coordinated movement toward the dragon pit, while WBG’s players regroup. The segment concludes with T1 controlling the river and preparing for the next objective, while WBG’s players reposition for a potential counter-push. segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with commentators discussing Zeus's cautious top lane play, noting he "plays it so far back, plays it calm, cool and collected" to allow T1 to gain "incremental advantages." The commentary highlights T1's significant "8000 gold lead" and "Rebel Baron power play," describing it as "gigantic" given the game's previously close state. They emphasize T1's historical ability to "break open games," suggesting a "good sign if you are a T1 fan," and remark that "Weibo [WBG] well and truly on the ropes here." Despite this audio assessment, the visual action shows WBG initiating a pivotal mid-game skirmish in the river. WBG’s Crisp and Light cautiously advance from the bottom, while Xiaohu and Weiwei push from mid and jungle. T1’s Keria and Gumayusi position defensively, with Faker and Oner rotating in. The fight erupts as WBG’s Weiwei engages Oner, securing first blood with a quick burst. TheShy, now level 11, joins from top, catching Zeus off-guard and securing a kill, even as commentators discuss the difficulty of "killing Zay [Zeus]" when he's strong. T1’s Faker, despite leveling up to 14, is pressured by Xiaohu and retreats. The skirmish intensifies with WBG’s Crisp using his ultimate to control the area, while T1’s Keria attempts to peel for Gumayusi. TheShy’s aggressive play forces Zeus to retreat (before his death), and WBG gains a slight advantage. Commentators note T1's coordination, stating "No one is actually segmenting themselves off from the rest, they're always working in tandem," and discuss winning 4v4s and 1v1s while keeping "eyes on Weibo." The minimap shows T1’s remaining members rotating, but WBG’s coordinated push maintains river control. The score remains close at 6-5 for T1, with a slight gold lead, but WBG’s aggressive, successful engagements suggest a momentum shift. The bottom-left player feeds show WBG players intensely focused, with Light and Crisp cautious, while T1’s Gumayusi and Keria appear tense as they retreat in the dark, atmospheric environment illuminated by combat effects and glowing banners.

segments.integrated_caption: At 26:28, a tense League of Legends match between WBG and T1 unfolds, with WBG trailing 5-10 in kills and 42.3K to T1's 50.3K gold. The action centers around the river and mid-lane as commentators discuss T1's potential moves, with one noting "it's gonna be a T1 move" and "Faker waiting over the wall wants to land that charm to start this fight potentially." WBG's Weiwei and Crisp engage T1's Oner and Keria in the river, leading to Weiwei securing first blood on Oner, while commentators observe "Weibo just back away, not wanting to take a fight unless it is far more within their favor and with more information." Simultaneously, in the top lane, TheShy trades with Zeus, ultimately securing a kill as Zeus attempts to retreat. Mid-lane sees Xiaohu gaining the upper hand against Faker, pushing the lane. The camera pans as T1's Gumayusi and Keria retreat from the river, while WBG's Light and Crisp maintain cautious positioning. Commentators highlight T1's strategic play, noting "knowing that T1 is on the reset" and their efforts to "reclaim some vision," while also anticipating key item timings like "the death cap is going to be here soon for Faker," "Wits End going to be pretty soon as well for Gooma," and "when the GA comes in for Oner as well." They conclude that "T1 are just trying to get as prepared as possible for the soul or for this Baron where they are really going to look to dominate," contrasting this with "how little Weibo know" about T1's jungle presence, making "retaking control incredibly difficult." The minimap shows T1 focusing river and mid, while WBG consolidates. Player feeds display WBG in black jerseys with 'ifland' and 'R.GG' logos, and T1 in white jerseys, including Faker with glasses and Zeus focused. By 27:22, WBG's gold increases to 43.5K and T1's to 52.0K, with Prime Gaming, OPPO, 'WORLDS 2023 EVENT PASS', and 'Secret Lab' advertisements visible amidst glowing 'THE GLORY' banners and spell effects in the river.

segments.integrated_caption: At 28:23, with T1 leading WBG 10-5 in kills and 54.2K to 45.2K gold, the game unfolds in the river near the blue side's dragon pit. Commentators discuss the need for poke to contest Baron, noting it's "all up to Xiaohu" to hit "shock blast on key members that can't heal them up," specifically "Faker." The action intensifies as WBG's Weiwei, visible with a focused expression in the bottom-left player feed, engages T1's Oner in the river. Commentators exclaim, "Oner going aggressive, there's the kick flash once again!" as Weiwei "flashes with his advance to get himself back," initiating a skill combo that roots Oner, leading to his death for first blood. The commentators note T1 "tried something" but only got the flash from the jungler, discussing how T1 "don't even have to give up Oner's GA." Simultaneously, Xiaohu (WBG mid laner) and Faker (T1 mid laner) trade blows in the mid-lane, with Xiaohu gaining an advantage and leveling up to 16, his "shock blast" eventually coming in and putting "fear into Weibo and Zeus." Crisp (WBG support) and Keria (T1 support), with Keria "looking for that angle," exchange abilities near the river, while Light (WBG ADC) and Gumayusi (T1 ADC) maintain cautious positioning. In the top lane, TheShy (WBG top laner) clashes with Zeus (T1 top laner), securing a kill on Zeus after a level 16 power spike. As the skirmish concludes, T1 regroups, with Faker and Keria leading the push towards the dragon. The minimap shows T1's coordinated movement, while WBG players retreat. Commentators observe "three hits this cloud drake" as T1 successfully slays the dragon at 29:17, confirmed by the on-screen notification 'Red team has slain the Dragon!' and the dragon’s health bar depleting. The environment features the river, jungle, and stone pathways with glowing 'THE GLORY' banners, while the UI displays scores, gold, and champion stats, with the Secret Lab logo visible and a brief Red Bull ad. Commentators then anticipate T1 "going to start Baron at the same time."
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment captures a pivotal moment in a League of Legends match between WBG and T1, with the game clock at 28:23 and T1 leading in kills (10 to 5) and gold (54.2K to 45.2K). The action unfolds in the river area near the blue side’s dragon pit, where a skirmish erupts between the two teams. WBG’s Weiwei (jungler) engages T1’s Oner (jungler) in the river, initiating the fight with a skill combo that results in Oner being rooted and ultimately killed for first blood. Xiaohu (WBG mid laner) and Faker (T1 mid laner) trade blows in the mid-lane, with Xiaohu gaining an advantage and leveling up to 16. Crisp (WBG support) and Keria (T1 support) exchange abilities near the river, while Light (WBG ADC) and Gumayusi (T1 ADC) maintain cautious positioning. TheShy (WBG top laner) and Zeus (T1 top laner) clash in the top lane, with TheShy securing a kill on Zeus after a level 16 power spike. As the fight concludes, T1 regroups and secures the dragon, with Faker and Keria leading the push. The minimap shows T1’s coordinated movement toward the dragon, while WBG’s players retreat. The bottom-left player feeds show Weiwei, Xiaohu, Light, Crisp, and TheShy with focused expressions, while the bottom-right feeds show Oner, Faker, Gumayusi, Keria, and Zeus reacting to the skirmish. The environment features the river, jungle, and stone pathways with glowing 'THE GLORY' banners, and the on-screen UI displays scores, gold, and champion stats. The Secret Lab logo is visible in the bottom-left corner, and the Red Bull 'GIVES YOU WIINGS.' ad appears briefly before being replaced by the Secret Lab logo. The game transitions into a dragon fight, with T1 successfully slaying the dragon at 29:17, as indicated by the on-screen notification 'Red team has slain the Dragon!' and the dragon’s health bar depleting to zero.

segments.integrated_caption: As the segment opens, the commentators' urgent voices declare T1's aggressive intent, stating they 'can do everything at once' and urging WBG to 'make a stand now' as T1 secures Baron Nashor, with on-screen text confirming 'Red team has slain Baron Nashor!'. The atmosphere is tense, underscored by the commentators' rapid-fire analysis of T1's Baron Power Play push towards the blue base. WBG's jungler, Weiwei, is noted to be 'in the vicinity' by the commentators, while TheShy, WBG's top laner, is mentioned to have 'teleport' but T1 lacks vision. Game sounds intensify as T1 advances, with the commentators noting 'This Baron is gone' and 'This can't walk up'. Xiaohu, WBG's mid laner, is described as 'pretty fast' and 'gonna avoid the charm for now' as he skirmishes with Faker, who is hit by a 'Shockwave'. With the Baron buff, T1, as seen on the minimap, coordinates a relentless push, feeling they 'don't need to reset' but instead 'break open the base of Wabo here in game one of the finals'. The commentators highlight 'Nature's Grasp flying forward' and Oner still having his 'GA'. The action escalates dramatically as Crisp, WBG's support, goes 'incredibly low', and visually, TheShy (WBG) levels up to 16 and eliminates Zeus (T1) near the base, despite a commentator's momentary miscall of 'Zeus just executes The Shy'. Simultaneously, Gumayusi (T1 ADC) destroys the blue inhibitor, prompting an excited commentator to exclaim, 'This guy's Yuni is just absurd!'. On-screen text confirms 'T1 Gumayusi has destroyed a blue inhibitor!'. Weiwei is seen 'burning there as well' as Xiaohu 'tries to be the hero', but the commentators conclude 'it does not work out', with T1 leading significantly in kills (10-5) and gold (55.8K vs 47.4K). Sponsor logos like 'Secret Lab', 'verizon', 'Red Bull', and 'BARON POWER PLAY' are visible throughout the high-stakes final moments.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment captures a pivotal moment in a League of Legends match between WBG and T1, centered around a Baron Nashor fight and subsequent push into the enemy base. The Red team (T1) successfully slays Baron Nashor, triggering a power play as they immediately push toward the blue side base. The in-game action intensifies as T1 secures the Baron buff and begins dismantling WBG’s defenses. T1’s ADC, Gumayusi, destroys the blue inhibitor, while their top laner, Zeus, is eliminated by TheShy in a skirmish near the base. TheShy, WBG’s top laner, is seen leveling up to 16 and securing a kill, while WBG’s jungler, Weiwei, and support, Crisp, engage in the fight near the river and base. The bottom-left player feeds show WBG players (Xiaohu, Light, Crisp, Weiwei, TheShy) intensely focused, with Xiaohu and Crisp visible during the fight. T1’s players (Faker, Oner, Gumayusi, Keria, Zeus) are shown in the bottom-right feeds, with Faker and Keria coordinating the push. The minimap reveals T1’s coordinated movement, while the scoreboard shows T1 leading in kills (10-5) and gold (55.8K vs 47.4K). The environment shifts from the river to the base, with T1’s Baron buff enabling them to destroy the blue inhibitor and push further. On-screen text announces 'Red team has slain Baron Nashor!' and 'Blue turret destroyed!', followed by 'T1 Gumayusi has destroyed a blue inhibitor!' The bottom-left corner displays sponsor logos including 'Secret Lab', 'verizon', 'Red Bull', and 'BARON POWER PLAY', while the bottom-right corner shows the minimap and player stats. The atmosphere is tense and fast-paced, with T1 capitalizing on the Baron buff to gain a significant advantage.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the climactic final moments of a League of Legends match, as two excited male commentators exclaim that T1 is "gonna end game one here." Visually, T1 executes a dominant Baron power play, with Oner achieving a rampage and Gumayusi securing a kill on WBG's Xiaohu, leading to the destruction of a blue turret and a final push through the base. The commentators highlight how T1, despite an early game struggle, found the crucial skirmish, took the Baron, and from that point, "Weibo doesn't get to fight back." The on-screen text 'VICTORY' appears, followed by 'T1 LEADS SERIES 1-0', confirming T1's win. The commentators declare T1 "one and zero here in the World Final," walking away victorious. The camera cuts to the T1 players at their blue-lit desk, visibly relieved and composed, with Faker adjusting his headset and standing up. A wide shot reveals the arena, filled with a loudly cheering crowd under dramatic blue stage lighting, with large screens displaying player reactions. The commentators analyze T1's "early game composition" and its "very well" execution, while also noting "angles for Weibo to go back and try and reckon and figure it out for game two." T1 players, in their white jerseys, then walk off the stage, passing the large, silver Championship Trophy, while the WBG players, in black jerseys, follow in a subdued manner, as a commentator acknowledges WBG's tough situation.
